Title: Clean Code
Author: Robert C. Martin
Description: Practical advice on writing readable, maintainable code with refactoring examples and coding principles.

Title: The Pragmatic Programmer
Author: Andrew Hunt
Description: Guides developers to think pragmatically about coding, design, and career-long craftsmanship.

Title: Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software
Author: Gang of Four
Description: Classic catalog of reusable object-oriented design patterns with examples in C++ and Smalltalk.

Title: Introduction to Algorithms
Author: Thomas H. Cormen
Description: Comprehensive reference on algorithms with theory, proofs, and pseudocode for many core topics.

Title: The Mythical Man-Month
Author: Frederick P. Brooks Jr.
Description: Essays on software project management, team communication, and the limits of adding more people.

Title: Code Complete
Author: Steve McConnell
Description: Detailed guide to construction-level coding techniques, style, and quality for large codebases.

Title: Refactoring: Improving the Design of Existing Code
Author: Martin Fowler
Description: Shows step-by-step refactorings to make legacy code cleaner, safer, and easier to extend.

Title: Head First Design Patterns
Author: Eric Freeman
Description: Visual, example-heavy introduction to design patterns for beginners and intermediate coders.

Title: You Don't Know JS: Scope & Closures
Author: Kyle Simpson
Description: Short deep-dive into JavaScript scope, closures, and how the language handles variables.

Title: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
Author: Harold Abelson
Description: Foundational text exploring programming concepts, abstraction, and interpreters with Scheme.

Title: The Art of Computer Programming, Vol. 1
Author: Donald E. Knuth
Description: Advanced reference on algorithms, mathematical analysis, and fundamental programming techniques.

Title: Domain-Driven Design
Author: Eric Evans
Description: Explains how to design complex systems by modeling rich domains with ubiquitous language.

Title: Working Effectively with Legacy Code
Author: Michael Feathers
Description: Techniques for adding tests and refactoring legacy systems without breaking existing behavior.

Title: Continuous Delivery
Author: Jez Humble
Description: Describes practices for reliable, frequent software releases through automation and testing.

Title: The Clean Coder
Author: Robert C. Martin
Description: Focuses on professional habits, ethics, and discipline needed to be a responsible developer.

Title: Cracking the Coding Interview
Author: Gayle Laakmann McDowell
Description: Interview preparation book with coding questions, solutions, and tips for technical interviews.

Title: Algorithms, Part I
Author: Robert Sedgewick
Description: Introduction to data structures and elementary algorithms with an emphasis on Java code.

Title: C# in Depth
Author: Jon Skeet
Description: Explores advanced C# language features and how to use them effectively in real projects.

Title: Pro C# 10 with .NET 6
Author: Andrew Troelsen
Description: Comprehensive guide to modern C# and .NET 6, covering language, libraries, and application types.

Title: Agile Principles, Patterns, and Practices in C#
Author: Robert C. Martin
Description: Applies agile principles and design patterns to C# projects with case studies and examples.

Title: Head First Java
Author: Kathy Sierra
Description: Beginner-friendly introduction to Java using visual explanations and practical exercises.

Title: Effective Java
Author: Joshua Bloch
Description: Collection of best practices and design tips for writing robust, efficient Java code.

Title: Java Concurrency in Practice
Author: Brian Goetz
Description: Deep dive into building correct and performant concurrent programs in Java.

Title: Patterns of Enterprise Application Architecture
Author: Martin Fowler
Description: Catalog of patterns for building large-scale enterprise systems with layered architectures.

Title: Test-Driven Development: By Example
Author: Kent Beck
Description: Introduces TDD through simple examples, showing how tests drive design and refactoring.

Title: Extreme Programming Explained
Author: Kent Beck
Description: Describes Extreme Programming values and practices for small, agile development teams.

Title: The Phoenix Project
Author: Gene Kim
Description: Novel about IT operations, DevOps, and improving flow in a struggling company.

Title: The DevOps Handbook
Author: Gene Kim
Description: Practical guide to implementing DevOps practices for faster, safer software delivery.

Title: Soft Skills: The Software Developer's Life Manual
Author: John Sonmez
Description: Covers career, productivity, learning, and life skills for software developers.

Title: The Linux Programming Interface
Author: Michael Kerrisk
Description: Detailed reference to Linux system calls, APIs, and low-level programming concepts.

Title: Operating System Concepts
Author: Abraham Silberschatz
Description: Standard textbook covering processes, memory, file systems, and OS design principles.

Title: Computer Networks
Author: Andrew S. Tanenbaum
Description: Explains networking concepts from physical layer up to applications with clear diagrams.

Title: Compilers: Principles, Techniques, and Tools
Author: Alfred V. Aho
Description: Known as the Dragon Book, covers theory and practice of compiler design and implementation.

Title: Clean Architecture
Author: Robert C. Martin
Description: Guides architects on separating concerns and designing systems with clear boundaries.

Title: The Linux Command Line
Author: William E. Shotts Jr.
Description: Beginner-friendly introduction to Linux shell usage and scripting with practical examples.

Title: Python Crash Course
Author: Eric Matthes
Description: Hands-on introduction to Python with exercises and small projects for beginners.

Title: Fluent Python
Author: Luciano Ramalho
Description: Explores advanced Python features and idioms to write clear, powerful Pythonic code.

Title: Automate the Boring Stuff with Python
Author: Al Sweigart
Description: Shows how to use Python scripts to automate everyday tasks like files, Excel, and web data.

Title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
Author: Martin Kleppmann
Description: Explains the principles behind scalable, reliable data systems and modern storage technologies.

Title: SQL Antipatterns
Author: Bill Karwin
Description: Shows common database design and query mistakes and how to fix them for better SQL.

Title: Seven Databases in Seven Weeks
Author: Eric Redmond
Description: Tours different database models, from relational to NoSQL, with practical examples.

Title: Head First SQL
Author: Lynn Beighley
Description: Friendly visual guide to learning SQL queries, joins, and database design basics.

Title: JavaScript: The Good Parts
Author: Douglas Crockford
Description: Focuses on the most reliable and elegant features of JavaScript for building robust code.

Title: Eloquent JavaScript
Author: Marijn Haverbeke
Description: Modern introduction to JavaScript and web programming with exercises and projects.

Title: You Don't Know JS: Async & Performance
Author: Kyle Simpson
Description: Explains asynchronous patterns and performance tuning in JavaScript.

Title: Learning React
Author: Alex Banks
Description: Introduces modern React concepts like components, hooks, and state management.

Title: You Don't Know JS: ES6 & Beyond
Author: Kyle Simpson
Description: Covers new JavaScript language features from ES6 and later editions.

Title: Pro ASP.NET Core MVC
Author: Adam Freeman
Description: Step-by-step guide to building web applications with ASP.NET Core MVC.

Title: Pro WPF in C#
Author: Matthew MacDonald
Description: Covers building rich desktop applications using WPF, XAML, and C#.

Title: Windows Presentation Foundation Unleashed
Author: Adam Nathan
Description: Hands-on guide to creating UI with WPF, including layout, controls, and data binding.

Title: Head First HTML and CSS
Author: Elisabeth Robson
Description: Friendly visual guide to building web pages with HTML and CSS from scratch.

Title: CSS: The Definitive Guide
Author: Eric A. Meyer
Description: Detailed reference to CSS layout, selectors, and advanced styling techniques.

Title: Don't Make Me Think
Author: Steve Krug
Description: Short, practical guide to web usability and simple user-centered design.

Title: The Design of Everyday Things
Author: Don Norman
Description: Explores how design choices help or hinder users, with examples from daily objects.

Title: About Face: The Essentials of Interaction Design
Author: Alan Cooper
Description: Comprehensive look at interaction design patterns and user-centered interfaces.

Title: Lean UX
Author: Jeff Gothelf
Description: Shows how to integrate UX design into agile teams with experiments and feedback.

Title: Refactoring UI
Author: Adam Wathan
Description: Practical visual design tips for developers who want better-looking interfaces.

Title: The Manager's Path
Author: Camille Fournier
Description: Explores roles from tech lead to CTO and how to grow as an engineering leader.

Title: Peopleware
Author: Tom DeMarco
Description: Discusses the human side of software projects, focusing on teams and work environments.

Title: Team Topologies
Author: Matthew Skelton
Description: Describes patterns for organizing software teams for fast flow and clear ownership.

Title: Code: The Hidden Language of Computer Hardware and Software
Author: Charles Petzold
Description: Explains how computers work from basics like electricity up to operating systems.

Title: Grokking Algorithms
Author: Aditya Bhargava
Description: Illustrated introduction to common algorithms for beginners using plain language.

Title: Head First Design Patterns, 2nd Edition
Author: Eric Freeman
Description: Updated edition of the visual guide to design patterns using modern examples.

Title: C Programming Language
Author: Brian W. Kernighan
Description: Classic text introducing the C language and structured programming techniques.

Title: Programming Pearls
Author: Jon Bentley
Description: Collection of essays on programming problems, data structures, and clever solutions.

Title: The Little Schemer
Author: Daniel P. Friedman
Description: Playful introduction to recursion and functional programming using Scheme.

Title: Designing Interfaces
Author: Jenifer Tidwell
Description: Pattern collection for common user interface problems on desktop and web.

Title: Lean Software Development
Author: Mary Poppendieck
Description: Applies lean manufacturing principles to software development and project management.

Title: Agile Estimating and Planning
Author: Mike Cohn
Description: Explains how to estimate, plan, and track work in agile projects realistically.

Title: Scrum: The Art of Doing Twice the Work in Half the Time
Author: Jeff Sutherland
Description: Introduction to Scrum with stories about improving productivity and teamwork.

Title: The Art of Unit Testing
Author: Roy Osherove
Description: Shows how to write good unit tests and structure code for testability.

Title: xUnit Test Patterns
Author: Gerard Meszaros
Description: Comprehensive catalog of patterns for unit tests and test architectures.

Title: Continuous Integration
Author: Paul M. Duvall
Description: Explains the practices and tools behind continuous integration in software teams.

Title: Release It!
Author: Michael T. Nygard
Description: Discusses patterns and pitfalls of real-world production systems and reliability.

Title: The Art of Scalability
Author: Martin L. Abbott
Description: Shows how to scale both software systems and organizations as usage grows.

Title: High Performance Browser Networking
Author: Ilya Grigorik
Description: Explains how browsers use networks and how to optimize web performance.

Title: Web Performance in Action
Author: Jeremy Wagner
Description: Practical techniques to speed up websites, from images to caching and scripts.

Title: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn and TensorFlow
Author: Aurélien Géron
Description: Practical introduction to machine learning using Python libraries and examples.

Title: Deep Learning
Author: Ian Goodfellow
Description: Textbook covering theory and practice of deep neural networks and representation learning.

Title: Grokking Deep Learning
Author: Andrew Trask
Description: Friendly introduction to building neural networks from first principles in Python.

Title: The Pragmatic Programmer, 20th Anniversary Edition
Author: Andrew Hunt
Description: Updated edition of the classic book on pragmatic software craftsmanship.

Title: Clean Agile
Author: Robert C. Martin
Description: Explores agile values and practices from the perspective of the Clean Code author.

Title: Refactoring to Patterns
Author: Joshua Kerievsky
Description: Shows how to refactor existing code to introduce classic design patterns safely.

Title: Growing Object-Oriented Software, Guided by Tests
Author: Steve Freeman
Description: Demonstrates building an application incrementally using TDD and good OO design.

Title: Working in Public: The Making and Maintenance of Open Source Software
Author: Nadia Eghbal
Description: Looks at how open source projects are built, maintained, and funded today.

Title: Open Source Licensing
Author: Andrew M. St. Laurent
Description: Explains common open source licenses and what they mean for developers and users.

Title: Practical Object-Oriented Design in Ruby
Author: Sandi Metz
Description: Teaches clean, flexible OO design through Ruby-based examples and refactoring.

Title: Ruby on Rails Tutorial
Author: Michael Hartl
Description: Project-based introduction to building web applications with Ruby on Rails.

Title: The Go Programming Language
Author: Alan A. A. Donovan
Description: Comprehensive introduction to Go language syntax, concurrency, and tooling.

Title: Programming Rust
Author: Jim Blandy
Description: Teaches Rust's ownership model, safety guarantees, and systems programming features.

Title: Effective C++
Author: Scott Meyers
Description: Collection of guidelines and idioms for writing robust, efficient C++ code.

Title: More Effective C++
Author: Scott Meyers
Description: Follow-up volume with additional C++ tips, pitfalls, and advanced topics.

Title: The Rust Programming Language
Author: Steve Klabnik
Description: Official Rust book covering language basics, ownership, and building applications.

Title: Practical Vim
Author: Drew Neil
Description: Tips and workflows for becoming productive with the Vim text editor.

Title: Mastering Emacs
Author: Mickey Petersen
Description: Guide to using Emacs effectively as a powerful, extensible development environment.

Title: The Unix Programming Environment
Author: Brian W. Kernighan
Description: Classic introduction to Unix philosophy, tools, and shell scripting.

Title: Site Reliability Engineering
Author: Betsy Beyer
Description: Describes Google's SRE practices for running reliable, large-scale services.

Title: The Site Reliability Workbook
Author: Betsy Beyer
Description: Companion book with practical case studies and exercises in SRE practices.

Title: Chaos Engineering
Author: Casey Rosenthal
Description: Explains how to experiment on systems to build confidence in their resilience.

Title: The Art of Logging
Author: Fictional Author
Description: Introductory guide to designing useful logs and monitoring behavior in applications.
